readings.title: Attention Is All You Need
readings.note: The original transformer paper. Read sections 1-3 carefully — the architecture described here is the foundation of every modern LLM. Pay particular attention to the scaled dot-product attention derivation and the multi-head attention formulation.

readings.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
readings.note: OpenAI's empirical study establishing power-law relationships between model performance and compute, data, and parameters. Key insight: performance is predictable as a function of scale, enabling informed decisions about training budgets.

readings.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models (Chinchilla)
readings.note: DeepMind's correction to Kaplan's scaling laws, demonstrating that most LLMs were undertrained. The Chinchilla-optimal ratio of roughly 20 training tokens per parameter reshaped how the industry trains models.

readings.title: Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units
readings.note: The paper that introduced Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) for NLP tokenization. Understanding BPE is essential for reasoning about token counts, costs, and model behavior on different types of input.

readings.title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
readings.note: The key systems paper that made long-context LLMs practical. FlashAttention computes exact attention without materializing the full n×n attention matrix in HBM, achieving 2-4x speedups. Understanding this helps explain why context windows have grown dramatically.
